own_review.issue: Typo in user-facing description: 'stanard input/output' should be 'standard input/output'. Central to the PR's purpose (fixing generic/wrong descriptions), so shipping a new typo defeats the point.
Terminology: 'server side events (SSE)' should be 'Server-Sent Events (SSE)' (the standard name). Same wrong term appears in the sse quarkus-extension.yaml name (line 1).

Guide URL question: new yamls set guide to https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html while core/runtime's quarkus-extension.yaml deliberately keeps the guide commented out and with a different path (/mcp-server/dev/). Confirm the guide is published and align core for consistency (human confirmed it is published and suggested this exact URL).

Consistency: core's quarkus-extension.yaml has an explicit top-level 'description:' field; the two new yamls omit it and rely on the <description> placed in the parent pom being inherited by the runtime module. Adding description to the yaml (or runtime pom) would match the core convention and be more robust.
Blank line removed where description was inserted; purely cosmetic.

points.note: Verified real; matches human reference point 3.

points.point: Both new yamls omit top-level description field present in core yaml; consistency question
points.note: Verified: core yaml has 'description:', new yamls do not. Real consistency issue beyond human.

points.point: Guide URL active in new files but commented out in core; verify docs live / consider uncommenting core
points.note: Verified: core yaml has '#  guide: ...' commented. Matches the human's guide-URL concern (human confirmed guide is published).

missed: 'server side events' should be 'Server-Sent Events' in sse pom description and sse yaml name
exceeds: yaml description-field consistency with core extension

points.note: Verified real; matches human reference point 3.

points.point: New SSE/stdio yamls lack the top-level description field that core yaml has; consistency question
Guide URL differs from core's commented-out URL in both path structure (/mcp-server/ vs /quarkus-mcp-server/) and commented vs uncommented; confirm correctness and align core
points.note: Verified: core has '#  guide: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/mcp-server/dev/'. More precise than v5's version of the same point; matches the human's guide-URL concern.

missed: 'server side events' should be 'Server-Sent Events' in sse pom description and sse yaml name
exceeds: yaml description-field consistency with core extension
explicit identification of the path discrepancy between core's commented guide URL and the new URLs

qualitative: Both candidates caught the 'stanard' typo (the human's concrete fix), raised the guide-URL question that maps onto the human's main comment, and independently surfaced the real yaml description-field inconsistency with the core extension — all three points verified against the repo, with zero fabrications on either side. Both missed the 'server side events' vs 'Server-Sent Events' terminology error in the SSE pom description and yaml name, which is squarely within this metadata-focused PR's scope. v7's guide-URL point is meaningfully sharper than v5's: it pinpoints the path discrepancy (/mcp-server/dev/ vs /quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html) rather than just the commented/uncommented status. Nets tie at 2; on equal scores v7 edges ahead on precision, with v5 slightly more hedged phrasing but equally accurate claims.
